title: Remove remaining Router dependencies and delete module
description: Migrate runtime callsites from Zaq.Channels.Router to NodeRouter.dispatch + Channels.Api actions, then remove lib/zaq/channels/router.ex and update tests/docs.
notes: Partial progress complete (NodeRouter recursion + Agent/Channels bridge callback path + channel suites green), but full Router removal is blocked by remaining runtime/UI dependencies: lib/zaq/channels/router.ex still exists and active references remain in chat_live, notification_imap_live, supervisor, identity_plug, and agent pipeline/executor typing path. Requires coordinated API migration plan for send_typing/profile/runtime sync ownership before safe deletion.

title: Channels API: call bridge implementation callbacks
description: Ensure Channels.Api actions resolve bridge implementation modules and invoke callbacks directly (send_reply and related callbacks). Channels.Api must not call CommunicationBridge for outgoing delivery orchestration.
close_reason: Channels.Api now resolves bridge implementations and invokes bridge callbacks directly for outgoing delivery orchestration; channels API and bridge tests passing.

title: Agent API: persist then set response+next_hop
description: Refactor Agent.Api response path to stop direct re-dispatch calls. Persist response context first, then return the same event with response payload and next_hop targeting channels deliver_outgoing action.
close_reason: Agent API now persists response context first and returns event with response + next_hop for channels deliver_outgoing; agent API tests passing.

title: Add recursive next-hop management in NodeRouter
description: Update NodeRouter.dispatch to move current next_hop into hops before dispatch, then recursively dispatch when returned event contains a new next_hop, preserving trace_id and hop history.
close_reason: Implemented recursive next-hop management in NodeRouter with hop history and trace propagation; node_router tests passing.

title: Channels hop-routing pass gate
description: Tracks the second-pass refactor: hop-recursive NodeRouter dispatch, agent event mutation for response forwarding, Channels.Api calling bridge implementations directly, and Router module removal.

title: Use shared bridge modules inside jido/email implementations
description: Refactor JidoChatBridge and EmailBridge to consume (`use Zaq.Channels.Bridge` etc..) shared Bridge and CommunicationBridge functions directly where applicable, keeping provider-specific logic only.

title: Move deliver_outgoing into shared bridge layer
description: Centralize outgoing delivery orchestration into shared communication_bridge module and remove duplicated deliver_outgoing paths in implementation bridges.
notes: Also make sure it provides the type: :async option to the Event.new/3 call so the outgoing delivery is done asynchronously

title: Finish removing persistence from communication channels
description: Remove remaining bridge-side response persistence calls and ensure response persistence is triggered at Agent level before return-hop dispatch to channels.

title: Channels follow-up alignment plan gate
description: Tracks the follow-up plan to finish channels refactor alignment after initial bridge/router consolidation. Scope: persistence ownership finalization, shared deliver_outgoing centralization, and shared module usage in jido/email bridges.

title: Regression tests and docs for channels bridge refactor
description:
Objective
Lock regression coverage and documentation for the channels bridge/router refactor and async hop flow.

Test matrix
- Incoming flow: adaptor -> bridge -> NodeRouter -> bridge -> adaptor
- Outgoing flow: NodeRouter -> API -> bridge -> adaptor
- Bridge implementations covered: JidoChatBridge, EmailBridge, WebBridge

Coverage expectations
- Async ACK behavior from incoming hop responses
- Same-adapter reply semantics
- Channels.Api action routing for outgoing and runtime sync
- Router removal verification (no functional path relies on Zaq.Channels.Router)

Documentation updates
- docs/services/channels.md: updated module map and flow definitions
- docs/architecture.md: updated event flow references for channels role boundary
- execution plan decision log updates if implementation choices shift

Done criteria
- Integration tests passing for key branches
- mix precommit passing
- docs aligned with final runtime behavior

title: Agent response return path through NodeRouter dispatch
description:
Objective
Update agent executor/API event shaping so responses are emitted as async hop events and dispatched with NodeRouter.dispatch to Channels destination bridge.

Target flow
Outgoing message from Zaq: NodeRouter -> API -> bridge -> adaptor

Scope
- Agent executor edits event to declare a new async hop for channel return path.
- Agent API persists response context via NodeRouter to Engine before dispatching return hop.
- Agent API fires response event to NodeRouter.dispatch.
- Channels boundary receives and delivers via bridge without router indirection.
- Preserve same-adapter reply semantics from original incoming message context.

Explicit migration decision
- Move response persistence ownership out of channel bridges.
- Remove bridge-level persist_from_incoming calls from JidoChatBridge and EmailBridge response path.
- Persistence must happen at Agent level before sending response back to channels.

Validation focus
- Default pipeline and explicit selection paths both emit valid return hops.
- Persistence dispatch happens before return-hop dispatch.
- Missing/invalid return destination metadata handling.
- Response reaches correct implementation bridge.

title: Move runtime routing into Channels API and remove Router
description:
Objective
Move runtime/outgoing routing ownership into Zaq.Channels.Api and remove Zaq.Channels.Router after migration.

Scope
- Channels.Api resolves destination bridge directly (using CommunicationBridge helpers).
- Replace Router call sites with Channels.Api action handling.
- Remove Router module once all usages are migrated.

Channels.Api actions in scope
- handle_event/3 action: deliver_outgoing
- handle_event/3 action: incoming_async_hop (new)
- handle_event/3 action: sync_channel_runtime (new/renamed as needed)
- handle_event/3 action: invoke (kept for internal boundary calls)

Validation focus
- Outgoing delivery success/failure through Api
- Unsupported action handling remains explicit
- No remaining runtime/outgoing call path through Zaq.Channels.Router

title: Adopt async HopEvent NodeRouter path for incoming ACK
description:
Objective
Adopt async HopEvent incoming flow so adapters receive ACK from NodeRouter response and bridges do not loop a reply back through NodeRouter.

Target flow
Incoming message: adaptor -> bridge -> NodeRouter -> bridge -> adaptor

Scope
- All incoming adapter messages dispatch via async hop event.
- Bridge returns NodeRouter response as ACK to adapter.
- Bridge no longer routes back to NodeRouter to send a reply path.
- Reply is expected to return through the same adapter destination metadata path.

Cross-issue constraint
- Response persistence is NOT owned by bridges in this flow.
- Bridge-level persist_from_incoming calls for response handling are removed; persistence is handled by Agent-level flow before return-hop dispatch.

Bridge orchestration touchpoints
- route_incoming/4 executes incoming lifecycle.
- before_incoming/4 and after_incoming/5 hooks apply.
- ack_from_event_response/1 provides adapter ACK normalization.

Validation focus
- ACK on successful hop dispatch.
- Error ACK when dispatch fails.
- No secondary NodeRouter loop for reply send from incoming path.

title: Refactor Bridge into generic incoming pipeline hooks
description:
Objective
Refactor Zaq.Channels.Bridge to remain ZAQ-internal generic incoming orchestration with hook points for implementation bridges.

Scope
- Introduce a basic incoming routing layer in Bridge.
- Add before/after hooks that implementation bridges can override.
- Preserve callback contracts for bridge implementations.

Functions to keep/extend in Bridge
- to_internal/2 (callback contract)
- send_reply/2 (callback contract)
- run_pipeline_with_node_router/5
- persist_from_incoming/5
- put_agent_selection_assign/2
- route_incoming/4 (new generic incoming route entrypoint)
- before_incoming/4 (new hook, default pass-through)
- after_incoming/5 (new hook, default pass-through)
- ack_from_event_response/1 (new ACK normalizer)

Implementation bridge expectations
- JidoChatBridge, EmailBridge, WebBridge keep from_listener/3, to_internal/2, send_reply/2.
- Optional overrides for before_incoming/4 and after_incoming/5 only where behavior differs.

Validation focus
- Default hook pass-through behavior
- Override behavior for at least one implementation bridge
- Error propagation through route_incoming/4

title: Extract CommunicationBridge with routing delegation
description:
Objective
Create Zaq.Channels.CommunicationBridge to centralize communication-domain callbacks and adapter routing helpers that are currently spread across bridge/router paths.

Scope
- Move bridge resolution and provider normalization concerns out of routing call sites.
- Keep this module transport/domain-facing (not ZAQ pipeline orchestration).
- Provide reusable helpers consumed by Channels.Api and implementation bridges.

Functions to define/migrate
- bridge_for/1
- provider_to_bridge_key/1
- deliver/1
- send_typing/2
- add_reaction/4
- remove_reaction/5
- subscribe_thread_reply/3
- unsubscribe_thread_reply/3
- sync_config_runtime/2
- sync_provider_runtime/1
- test_connection/2
- fetch_connection_details/1
- fetch_channel_config/1
- fetch_any_channel_config/1
- resolve_bridge/1
- dispatch_provider_runtime_sync/2

Validation focus
- Atom/string provider handling
- No configured bridge behavior
- Runtime sync delegation behavior
- Connection details lookup success/failure

title: Channels flow baseline and target contract
description:
Define and freeze the channel routing contract so downstream issues can implement safely and consistently.

Scope
- Document baseline versus target contracts for incoming and outgoing message flow.
- Make event boundaries explicit between Channels.Api, Bridge, and CommunicationBridge.
- Define ACK semantics at adapter boundary and NodeRouter async dispatch boundary.
- Define minimal and optional Event and EventHop fields for return-path routing.
- Publish a responsibility matrix and per-issue validation checklist for:
  - zaq-crk
  - zaq-4to
  - zaq-dyc
  - zaq-gpj
  - zaq-ohp
  - zaq-7il

Contract summary
- Baseline incoming flow: adaptor -> bridge -> NodeRouter -> bridge -> NodeRouter/API/router -> bridge -> adaptor.
- Target incoming flow: adaptor -> bridge -> NodeRouter -> bridge -> adaptor.
- Target outgoing flow: NodeRouter -> API -> bridge -> adaptor.
- Target ACK semantics: inbound ACK is async hop dispatch ACK (accepted or failed dispatch), not full pipeline completion ACK.

Event contract requirements
Minimum required
- Event.request present with canonical payload (Incoming for ingress, Outgoing for delivery).
- Event.next_hop.destination present and explicit.
- Event.next_hop.type set to async for inbound ACK/return-path flow.
- Event.opts includes explicit action gate.

Return-path required payload fields
- Incoming requires content, channel_id, provider.
- Outgoing requires body, channel_id, provider.
- Threaded channels require thread_id and/or in_reply_to where supported.

Optional but required by channel behavior
- trace_id for async hop correlation.
- actor and assigns for explicit context propagation.
- message_id as reply anchor.
- metadata for channel-specific routing (web session/request ids, email thread keys and threading headers).

Responsibility matrix
- CommunicationBridge: communication-domain routing/delegation helpers, provider normalization, bridge lookup, connection detail fetch, adapter dispatch.
- Bridge: behavior contract and ZAQ-internal generic incoming orchestration helpers.
- Channels.Api: NodeRouter role boundary with explicit action-gated handling for channel operations.

Acceptance criteria
- Baseline and target flows are explicit and unambiguous.
- ACK semantics are explicit at adapter boundary.
- Event/EventHop required fields are explicit.
- Module responsibilities are non-overlapping.
- Validation checklist maps one-to-one to each dependent issue.

Out of scope
- No code changes.
- No function-level migration steps.
- No implementation sequencing beyond contract-level validation gates.
